title: Bor Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bor Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Bor Tiger Reserve is a wildlife sanctuary which was declared as a tiger reserve in July 2014.
overview.description: Bor Tiger Reserve is a wildlife sanctuary which was declared as a tiger reserve in July 2014. It is located near Hingani in Wardha District in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is a home to a variety of wild animals. The reserve covers an area of 138.12 km2 (53.33 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Bor Dam
topPlaces.description: Bor Dam, is an earthfill dam on Bor river near Bori in the Bor Wildlife Sanctuary, Seloo Tahsil, Wardha district in State of Maharashtra in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bor Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bor Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bor Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport, Nagpur (41 km) and the nearest railway station is Nagpur Junction (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bor Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bor Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bor Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bor Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bor Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bor Dam.
